title: Bureau Veritas Minerals Pty Ltd — Public safety
investigation_objective: Bureau Veritas Minerals Pty Ltd pleaded guilty to a charge under South Australia's Work Health and Safety Act 2012 and was fined $210,000 (reduced 40% from $350,000 for an early guilty plea) plus costs and a Victims of Crime Levy, after a 20-year-old worker employed only a few weeks had a gloved hand pulled into a rock crusher's rollers at a Wingfield workshop in June 2023, amputating part of a finger. SafeWork SA found the company failed to provide an adequate interlock device, failed to assess energisation risk during clearing tasks, and gave inadequate training and supervision.
scope: Pleaded guilty to a work health and safety offence following a worker's serious crush injury; Regulator found the company failed to provide an adequate machine interlock device and gave inadequate training and supervision; Fined $210,000 plus costs by the SA Employment Court
